- Profile

ATD is a supplier of tires to the replacement tire market. It operates more than 130 distribution centers, including 24 distribution centers in Canada, serving approximately 80,000 customers across the U.S. and Canada. ATD was established in 1935 and is based in Huntersville, North Carolina.

results.title: Docebo Acquires 365Talents for $54 Million - HR Tech Feed
results.author: HR Tech Guy
results.text:
Docebo Acquires 365Talents for $54 Million - HR Tech Feed 

x

 

 

Read Time3 Minute, 27 Second

TORONTO & LYON, France—Docebo Inc. a leading AI-powered enterprise learning platform, today announced it has acquired all of the issued and outstanding shares of privately held 365Talents, an AI-powered skills intelligence and workforce analytics company based in France for $54 million and other considerations.

The Transaction is expected to strengthen Docebo’s ability to help organizations identify, develop, and deploy skills at scale by embedding skills intelligence more directly into learning workflows. The Transaction advances Docebo’s AI-powered workforce readiness approach by enabling the detection and automatic addressing of skill gaps through learning and talent actions tied directly to business needs.

“In today’s economy, skills only matter if they can be used,” said Alessio Artuffo, President and CEO of Docebo. “When skills sit on a shelf or live in static models, they quickly lose value. Most vendors help organizations collect and analyze skills, but very few help them act on them. At Docebo, we see skills as the input: learning, behavior change, and workforce outcomes are the real measure of success. By bringing 365Talents into Docebo, we’re using AI agents to turn skills into a living capability that drives learning, career mobility, and workforce decisions in real time. The result is a more coherent, measurable way for enterprises to develop people and adapt as work continuously evolves.”

365Talents brings deep expertise in AI-driven skills intelligence, applying autonomous agents and machine learning to continuously map, infer, and manage skills across the workforce. Its platform helps enterprises understand gaps and guide employees toward roles, projects, and development opportunities in a more dynamic way.

 See also Jobcase Acquires Recruitology

Individually, skills intelligence and learning platforms deliver value. Together, they create a multiplier effect. By combining Docebo’s AI-powered learning platform with 365Talents’ agent-based skills intelligence, the combined company is creating a new, AI-native way to manage skills, from detection to development to deployment.

- Move from insight to action: Use skills intelligence to automatically trigger learning, mobility, and workforce actions as needs change.
- Close the loop between learning and outcomes: Continuously connect skills data, learning activity, and workforce signals so progress is measured by real capability and application, not just course completion or reporting.
- Build AI-powered learning on skills: Use AI agents to identify and address skill gaps, create personalized learning, and adapt content in real time based on proven capability needs, not clicks or completions.

“When we started 365Talents, our goal was to help organizations make skills visible and usable,” said Loïc Michel, Chief Executive Officer of 365Talents. “Joining Docebo allows us to

# Docebo Acquires 365Talents

By Tracxn Analyst Team • Last updated: January 23, 2026

Most viewed in 2019

365Talents

Acquired by

$54.6MJan 20, 2026

Docebo

## Deal Summary

On January 20, 2026, Docebo, a publicly traded cloud-based learning management system provider, acquired 365Talents, a talent management platform, for $54.6 million USD. The acquisition signals Docebo's ambition to expand its footprint in the HRTech market beyond learning management and into broader talent management solutions.

Docebo, founded in 2005 and based in Athens, Georgia, United States, aims to integrate 365Talents' AI-powered talent intelligence and workforce analytics capabilities into its existing learning platform. 365Talents, founded in 2015 and based in Lyon, France, specializes in matching employee skills with internal opportunities, leveraging data from HRIS and LinkedIn. The strategic rationale centers on creating a more holistic employee development and management ecosystem, integrating skills intelligence directly into learning workflows.

This acquisition enhances Docebo's competitive positioning by offering a more comprehensive suite of talent and learning solutions, potentially attracting a wider customer base seeking integrated HR technology. For 365Talents, joining Docebo provides access to greater resources and market reach. The deal reflects a broader trend of consolidation in the HRTech sector as companies strive to offer more comprehensive platforms. Docebo's CEO emphasized the importance of utilizing skills, not just collecting data, and this acquisition aims to facilitate learning, career mobility, and real-time workforce decisions through autonomous AI agents for dynamic skills mapping. The move is viewed as a "capital-efficient bet" to accelerate Docebo's AI capabilities.

## Recent M&A Activity in the Sector

The HRTech sector experienced a surge in M&A activity, with 173 acquisitions recorded in 2025. Recent deals highlight the dynamic market.

Phenom Acquires Included: Phenom, an AI-powered HR platform based in Pennsylvania, United States, acquired Included, a cloud-based diversity recruiting platform based in Redmond, Washington, United States. Included, founded in 2020, offers an AI-native people analytics platform. This acquisition enhances Phenom's AI-driven recruitment analytics and DEI offerings, providing deeper data insights for workforce planning.

Prometheus Acquires Webalo: Prometheus, an enterprise asset management (EAM) software company

# Vensure Acquires Distro

By Tracxn Analyst Team • Last updated: January 25, 2026

Most viewed in 2019

Distro

Acquired by

UndisclosedJan 21, 2026

Vensure

## Deal Summary

On January 21, 2026, Vensure, a human resource management services provider, acquired Distro, an online job board platform and applicant tracking system, in a business acquisition for an undisclosed amount. Vensure, founded in 2004 and based in the United States, aims to bolster its HRTech offerings through the integration of Distro's AI-powered recruitment capabilities. Distro, founded in 2015 and also based in the United States, provides a platform featuring AI resume vetting, structured video interviews, AI matching, and an AI recruiting assistant, along with contractor payment and EOR services for international hiring.

This acquisition allows Vensure to offer a more comprehensive, end-to-end solution for businesses seeking to streamline their recruitment processes. By integrating Distro's platform, Vensure can capture a larger share of the HRTech market, providing clients with advanced, AI-driven talent acquisition capabilities. This includes reducing time-to-hire, improving candidate quality, and scaling hiring globally. For Distro, the acquisition provides access to Vensure's extensive resources and broader market reach, allowing its technology to be deployed across Vensure's large client base.

The deal reflects Vensure's commitment to leveraging technology, particularly AI, to enhance its service delivery and client experience. This consolidation of disparate HR products into a single platform strengthens Vensure's position in the competitive HR Services and HRTech landscape. The HRTech sector is experiencing consolidation as larger players acquire specialized technologies to offer integrated solutions, driven by the increasing adoption of AI in HR processes. Vensure, as the largest privately held organization in the HR technology and service sector, serving over 161,000 businesses globally, is well-positioned to capitalize on this trend.

## Recent M&A Activity in the Sector

The HRTech sector has seen significant M&A activity, with 173 acquisitions recorded in 2025, continuing into early 2026.

Docebo Acquires 365Talents: On January 19, 2026, Docebo, based in Athens, Georgia, United States, acquired 365Talents, based in Lyon, France, for $54.6 million. 365Talents is an AI-powered skills intelligence and workforce analytics company. Docebo, a corporate learning management system provider, aims to integrate
